description:
Third subticket. Depends on .16.2.

The expansion pass currently rewrites Expr nodes in fn bodies. To support `test "name" do ... end`, macros must be able to produce *items* — fn defs, item-level lists.

## Scope

- macros::expand_program (or a new expand_items pass) handles Item::MacroCall:
  - reify each arg
  - run the macro body via interp
  - decode the returned Value back to either a single Item or a List of Items
  - splice the result into the enclosing module's items in place of the original MacroCall
- ast_value: extend value_to_expr (or add value_to_item) for the Item shapes that macros are likely to return — at minimum a reified fn def. Decode shape: {:fn, %{name: atom, ...}, [...]} or whatever convention we adopt.
- Re-walk: post-splice, run the standard fn-body expansion over the new items.

## Tests

- Macro that returns a quoted fn definition; the expanded AST contains a real Item::Fn.
- The defined fn is callable from the rest of the module.

## Out of scope

- Macros that produce defmodule/alias/import — file follow-up if needed.
- Exposed @__test_metadata__-style attribute side effects (those wait for general @attribute support).
title: Item-producing macros
